accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and accurate context about the AECA section 36(c) disapproval process is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the AECA-authority context is accurate and verifiable.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill blocks a specific proposed arms export to Israel.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The resolution is a standalone disapproval that prohibits an export; it does not amend or repeal existing law.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions or conditions; the summary correctly notes none exist.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective dates or sunsets; the summary correctly notes none.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the essential operative effect: prohibition of the specified 20,000-rifle export under the named transmittal.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the USML Category I, DDTC Transmittal 23-077, the AECA, M.R.D. Efram Investments Ltd, and the Israel National Police.

correct_figures.why: The $1,000,000 threshold, 20,000 rifles, 11.5-inch barrel, 5.56mm caliber, and March 24/27, 2025 dates all match the bill.
